description: Customer (rider)-facing flow for requesting, tracking, and canceling rides through the public API.

actors.name: Customer
actors.description: End user requesting and tracking a ride.

actors.name: Platform
actors.description: API server processing customer requests and surfacing status updates.

fields.name: pickup_location
fields.label: Pickup address and coordinates (latitude, longitude, address string).

fields.name: scheduled_at
fields.label: Optional future pickup time for scheduled rides (omit for immediate).

fields.name: notes
fields.label: Customer instructions or special requests for the driver.

fields.name: order_id
fields.label: Public order identifier returned after booking (used for tracking and cancellation).

fields.name: status
fields.label: Current ride status visible to the customer.

fields.name: driver_location
fields.label: Current driver coordinates for live map display (lat, lng, heading).

fields.name: eta_seconds
fields.label: Estimated time until driver arrives at pickup, in seconds.

rules.rule_01: A customer must have a valid account (authenticated session or API key) to create an order.
rules.rule_02: Pickup and drop-off locations are required; a return location is optional.
rules.rule_03: On order creation, a preliminary distance and time estimate is calculated and stored.
rules.rule_04: A tracking number is issued at creation and can be used to poll order status without authentication.
rules.rule_05: Customers can cancel an order at any time before it reaches in_progress status.
rules.rule_06: After in_progress, cancellation is subject to platform policy (not enforced here).
rules.rule_07: The customer receives real-time status updates via their subscribed channel or by polling the order endpoint.
rules.rule_08: Driver location (for live map) is surfaced only once an order is dispatched.

given: customer provides valid pickup and drop-off locations

then.description: Order is persisted with status created and customer reference.

ride_requested.result: Customer receives an order_id and tracking_number to follow their ride.

given: customer queries the order by order_id or tracking_number

then.description: Current status, driver location, ETA, and activity history are returned.

ride_tracked.result: Customer sees live ride status and driver position on the map.

ride_canceled_by_customer.result: Ride is terminated; customer is notified.

given: customer provides a scheduled_at timestamp in the future

then.description: Order is persisted with status created and scheduled_at set; dispatch is deferred until near the scheduled time.

scheduled_ride_created.result: Ride is booked for the future; customer receives confirmation.

ride_completed_confirmation.result: Customer receives completion confirmation and trip summary.

errors.message: The specified ride could not be found.
Pickup or drop-off location is invalid or missing.

events.name: order.created
events.description: Ride request is created by the customer.

related.reason: Customer app creates the initial order that enters the lifecycle.
Customer app subscribes to driver location for live map.
ETA to pickup is shown in the customer app.
Customer app communicates via the public REST API.

goals.description: Customer (rider)-facing flow for requesting, tracking, and canceling rides through the public API.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts

tradeoffs.reason: workflow steps must complete correctly before proceeding
